explanation: The statement "When prop_0, the robot should prop_1" implies that whenever 'prop_0' holds, 'prop_1' must also hold at that same time. This is a common interpretation of a 'whenever P, Q' or 'if P, then Q' pattern, which translates to a global implication.

The phrase "When prop_0, the robot should prop_1" indicates that whenever 'prop_0' is true, 'prop_1' must also be true at that exact same time. This is a continuous constraint. The 'G' (globally) operator ensures this condition holds at all times, and the '->' (implication) operator captures the 'if-then' relationship: if 'prop_0' is true, then 'prop_1' must be true.

The phrase "When prop_0, the robot should prop_1" signifies that whenever prop_0 is true, prop_1 must also be true at that same moment. This is a global implication, meaning it must hold true at all times. Therefore, the LTL formula G (prop_0 -> prop_1) correctly captures this relationship, stating "Globally, if prop_0 is true, then prop_1 must be true."

The phrase 'When prop_0, the robot should prop_1' implies that whenever 'prop_0' is true, 'prop_1' must also be true at that exact moment. This is a global implication, meaning it must hold true at all times. Thus, we use the 'G' (Globally) operator with an implication (->) between 'prop_0' and 'prop_1'.

The statement "When prop_0, the robot should prop_1" implies a global implication: whenever 'prop_0' is true, 'prop_1' must also be true at that same instant. This is best represented by a global always operator (G) combined with a material implication (->).
The phrase 'When A, B' implies that A occurring leads to B. In LTL, this is best represented as 'Globally (G) if prop_0 is true then prop_1 is true (prop_0 -> prop_1)'.

The statement "When prop_0, the robot should prop_1" implies that whenever prop_0 is true, prop_1 must also be true at that exact moment. This can be directly translated as a global implication: G (prop_0 -> prop_1).

The statement "When prop_0, the robot should prop_1" implies that whenever 'prop_0' is true, 'prop_1' must also be true at that same instant. This is a classic 'always if P then Q' pattern, which translates to G(P -> Q) in LTL. In this case, P is 'prop_0' and Q is 'prop_1'.

The phrase "When prop_0, the robot should prop_1" indicates that every time prop_0 is true, prop_1 must also be true simultaneously. This is a global implication, meaning it must hold true at all points in time. Therefore, it is translated as G (prop_0 -> prop_1).
The phrase "When prop_0, prop_1" is interpreted as a global implication, meaning that whenever prop_0 is true, prop_1 must also be true at that same moment.
